alerts: Small Craft Advisory — Maui Windward Waters until 2026-03-05 06:00 HST

actual.swell_character: short choppy ENE windswell + small NNW groundswell
actual.cloud_cover_notes: Predominantly low cloud layer (63-86% low). Mid/high minimal. Classic heavy trade wind cumulus suppressing thermal drive.

verdict.session_quality_desc: Got on water but conditions were very difficult — extreme near-shore turbulence, required 500m body drag to launch
verdict.equipment_notes: 3.4m was correct size for open water but gust ratio made it difficult to handle

observations.free_text: Wind direction was effectively side-offshore creating a massive wind shadow from shoreline trees. Even 1km offshore the conditions were extremely gusty and turbulent ranging 15-25kts. Dramatic wind line extending very far offshore — nearly impossible to keep kite flying inside the shadow. Body dragged 500m offshore before finding launchable conditions. Gusts made kite control very difficult even in open water.
anomalies: wind shadow extended 500m+ offshore from shoreline trees
dramatic hard wind line far from shore
side-offshore angle made normal beach launch impossible
heavy low cloud (75-92%) suppressed thermal drive throughout session
iK-TRRM predicted 44% clearing — actual was 75-92% overcast
new_patterns: isthmus venturi rotation: upwind ENE (62°) → Kanaha E (88°) = 26° southward bend
due-E wind at Kanaha = behind tree line, shadow projects northwest offshore
sensor reads 19.6kts GOOD but near-shore is unrideable — sensors are in open air above shadow
heavy low cloud (>70%) + side-offshore direction = double suppression: no thermal boost AND wind shadow

sensor_actuals.thermal_drive_actual: suppressed — heavy low cloud prevented solar heating of isthmus

detection_outcome.note: Algorithm built and committed after this debrief. Would have correctly flagged this session.

cloud_correlation.thermal_impact: Heavy low cloud suppressed solar heating of isthmus. Land temp stayed close to ocean temp (Δ1.9°C only). Reduced thermal drive meant trades were the only power source — no thermal boost to fill lulls in shadow zone.
cloud_correlation.compounding_effect: Wind shadow (E direction) + suppressed thermal (heavy cloud) = double hazard. Shadow zone had no thermal convection to mix through it. Dead zone extended further and lasted longer than it would on a sunny day.

cloud_thresholds_for_kanaha.lt_30_pct: Clear — full thermal drive, shadow zones mix out faster
cloud_thresholds_for_kanaha.30_to_60_pct: Partial cloud — moderate thermal, some shadow risk
cloud_thresholds_for_kanaha.60_to_80_pct: Heavy cloud — thermal suppressed, shadow zones persist longer
cloud_thresholds_for_kanaha.gt_80_pct: Overcast — minimal thermal, maximum shadow persistence, wind character more synoptic/gusty
